error_handling.overview: Error scenarios and recovery strategies for synthetic-human-tester. Errors are classified as infrastructure errors (MCP unavailable, app not running), environmental errors (timeout, network), or test failures (assertion mismatch, element not found). Infrastructure and environmental errors are reported but do not fail the test — they cause the environment to be skipped. Test failures are reported as failed steps.

scenario.name: mcp_not_available
scenario.trigger: Capability detection finds zero matching MCP tools for a requested environment.
scenario.classification: Infrastructure error — not a test failure.

action.step: Record the environment as skipped with reason: "MCP not available."
If this is the only requested environment: set overall status to mcp_unavailable.
If other environments are available: continue with those environments, set overall status to partial_passed (if all executed steps pass) or tests_failed (if any executed step fails).

user_guidance.description: When reporting MCP unavailability, provide actionable setup instructions so the user can resolve the issue themselves. Synthetic-human-tester is self-sufficient: it guides the user through fixing configuration problems.
user_guidance.guidance: Web testing requires a browser automation MCP server. To set it up: 1. Install a browser automation MCP (e.g., Playwright MCP) 2. Add the MCP server to your Roo Code MCP configuration 3. The MCP must expose tools matching the browser_* pattern (browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_snapshot, browser_type) 4. Once configured, re-run the smoke tests

Native app testing requires an Appium MCP or equivalent that exposes tools matching appium_* or accessibility_* (appium_find_element, appium_click, appium_get_page_source). Set up an Appium server, connect it to your target app, and add the MCP to your Roo Code configuration.

Desktop app testing requires Windows-MCP or an equivalent desktop automation MCP. To set it up: 1. Install Windows-MCP from GitHub: https://github.com/CursorTouch/Windows-MCP 2. Prerequisites: Python 3.13+, then `pip install uv && uvx windows-mcp serve` 3. Add to your Roo Code MCP configuration: `{ "mcpServers": { "windows-mcp": { "command": "uvx", "args": ["windows-mcp", "serve"] } } }` 4. The MCP must expose tools named: Snapshot, Click, Type (exact names, no prefix) 5. Screenshot is optional but recommended for visual baseline capture 6. Once configured, re-run the smoke tests

scenario.recovery: None. The user should verify manually, or install the required MCP and retry.

scenario.name: app_not_reachable
scenario.trigger: First navigation or connection attempt to an environment fails (connection refused, DNS error, timeout).
scenario.classification: Infrastructure error — not a test failure.

action.step: Retry the connection once after a short wait (5 seconds).
If still unreachable: skip all steps for this environment. Record as skipped with reason: "Application not reachable at [url/endpoint]. Verify the app is running."

user_guidance.description: When an app is unreachable, help the user troubleshoot. Common causes and fixes:
user_guidance.guidance: The web app is not responding at the configured base_url. Troubleshooting: 1. Verify the dev server is running (check terminal for the dev server process) 2. Confirm the base_url is correct (e.g., http://localhost:3000, not https or wrong port) 3. Check for port conflicts or firewall blocking 4. If using a different port than configured, update the base_url in your test plan

The native app is not reachable via Appium. Troubleshooting: 1. Verify the Appium server is running (check the Appium endpoint) 2. Confirm the app is installed and launchable on the target device/emulator 3. Check the app_id matches the installed application

The desktop app could not be launched or is not responding. Troubleshooting: 1. Verify the executable_path is correct (use forward slashes) 2. Confirm the application is installed and can be launched manually 3. Check if the app requires admin privileges or specific launch arguments 4. If launch_args are needed, add them to the desktop environment config

scenario.recovery: None at this level. The user should verify the app is running at the configured URL and retry.

scenario.name: authentication_failed
scenario.trigger: Login form submission does not result in an authenticated state (still on login page, error message appears).
scenario.classification: Environmental error — could be wrong credentials or app issue.

action.step: Check the post-login snapshot for error messages.
If an error message is found: record as skipped with reason "Authentication failed: [error message]".
If no error message but still on login page: record as skipped with reason "Authentication failed: login did not redirect away from login page."
Skip all remaining steps for this environment.

scenario.recovery: None. The user should verify the credentials are correct and the auth flow is working.

scenario.name: element_not_found
scenario.trigger: A target element from the "when" or "then" clause cannot be found in the accessibility snapshot.
scenario.classification: Test failure if the element should exist per the test plan. May be transient if the page is still loading.

action.step: Wait and retake the snapshot once (page may still be loading).
If element still not found: record as failed step with evidence — include the full accessibility snapshot for the relevant area of the page.
If stop_on_first_failure is true: stop execution. Otherwise: continue to next step.

scenario.evidence_required: Include: (1) the element that was searched for (role + accessible name), (2) the matching strategy used, (3) the relevant portion of the accessibility snapshot showing what IS present.

scenario.name: assertion_mismatch
scenario.trigger: The post-action snapshot does not match the expected state from the "then" clause.
scenario.classification: Test failure — the application is not behaving as expected.

action.step: Record as failed step with specific evidence: expected state, actual state, relevant snapshot excerpt.
If stop_on_first_failure is true: stop execution. Otherwise: continue to next step.

scenario.evidence_required: Include: (1) the "then" clause verbatim, (2) what was observed that contradicts it, (3) the accessibility snapshot excerpt showing the discrepancy.

scenario.name: mcp_tool_timeout
scenario.trigger: An MCP tool call does not return within the configured timeout (default 30 seconds).

action.step: Retry the same tool call once.
If retry also times out: record the step as failed with reason "MCP tool timed out after 2 attempts."
If this is a browser_navigate or initial connection: treat as app_not_reachable for the environment.

scenario.name: mcp_tool_error
scenario.trigger: An MCP tool returns an error response (not a timeout).

action.step: Retry the same tool call once.
If retry also errors: record the step as failed with reason "MCP tool error: [error message]."

scenario.name: unexpected_page_state
scenario.trigger: The page shows an error (500, 404), a blocking modal, or content that doesn't match any expected state.
scenario.classification: Depends on the severity — may be a test failure or an environmental issue.

action.step: If error page (500, 404, etc.): record as failed step with the error page content.
If blocking modal that can be dismissed: dismiss it and continue.
If unexpected but non-blocking content: note it but continue with the test. If it prevents the assertion from being verified, record as failed.

scenario.name: desktop_app_not_found
scenario.trigger: Desktop executable_path does not exist, or the app process fails to start, or the window never appears.
scenario.classification: Infrastructure error — not a test failure.

action.step: Verify the executable_path exists at the configured location.

Attempt to launch the app. If the process fails immediately or the window doesn't appear within the timeout: skip all desktop steps with reason "Application not reachable at [executable_path]. Verify the path is correct and the app can be launched."

user_guidance.guidance: The desktop application could not be launched. Troubleshooting: 1. Verify the executable_path is correct (use forward slashes: C:/Program Files/MyApp/app.exe) 2. Confirm the application is installed and can be launched manually 3. Check if the app requires admin privileges or specific launch arguments 4. If launch_args are needed, add them to the desktop environment config

scenario.recovery: None. The user should verify the app path and retry.

scenario.name: desktop_window_not_responding
scenario.trigger: Desktop app launched but the window is not responding, or Snapshot returns empty/incomplete accessibility tree.
scenario.classification: Environmental error — the app may be hung or not exposing accessibility data.

action.step: Wait and retry Snapshot once (app may still be initializing).
If Snapshot still returns empty or incomplete data: skip all desktop steps with reason "Desktop window not responding or not exposing accessibility data."

scenario.recovery: None. The user should verify the app is functioning and exposes UI Automation data.

scenario.name: screenshot_capture_failed
scenario.trigger: browser_take_screenshot or Screenshot tool is available but returns an error or times out.
scenario.classification: Best-effort failure — does not affect functional testing.

action.step: Retry the screenshot call once.
If retry also fails: note "Screenshot capture failed — VISUAL section omitted" but continue with functional testing. This is not a test failure.

scenario.recovery: None. Proceed with functional testing only.

scenario.name: no_actionable_test_environments
scenario.trigger: After capability detection, ZERO environments have available MCPs.
scenario.classification: The spawn prompt requested environments but none can be tested.

action.step: Set overall status to mcp_unavailable.
Report each requested environment as skipped with the specific reason (MCP not found).
Include guidance on what MCPs to install for each environment type.
